fact: On July 4, 1776, the Continental Congress adopted the Declaration of Independence.
On September 17, 1787, delegates to the Constitutional Convention signed the U.S. Constitution in Philadelphia.
On January 1, 1863, President Abraham Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation.
On August 18, 1920, the 19th Amendment was ratified, guaranteeing women the right to vote in the United States.
On December 17, 1903, the Wright brothers achieved the first successful powered airplane flights at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina.
On July 20, 1969, Apollo 11 landed on the Moon and Neil Armstrong became the first person to walk on its surface.
In April 1990, the Hubble Space Telescope was launched as the first mission in NASA's Great Observatories program.
On June 26, 1945, the United Nations Charter was signed in San Francisco, establishing the United Nations.
On April 7, 1948, the Constitution of the World Health Organization came into force, marking the agency’s founding.
On November 9, 1989, the Berlin Wall opened, and its fall became a defining symbol of the end of the Cold War.
On November 17, 1869, the Suez Canal opened, creating a sea-level route linking the Mediterranean and Red seas.
In 1901, the first Nobel Prizes were awarded in Stockholm and Oslo.
